symptoms.symptom_name: 6 cm linear volar-forearm skin laceration exposing subcutaneous fat

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A clean full-thickness incised wound to subcutaneous fat is the defining lesion; true depth is only assessable in person.

symptoms.symptom_name: Dark venous ooze that slows with firm direct pressure (no pulsatile spray)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Dark, non-pulsatile bleeding controllable with pressure is venous/capillary, not arterial.
Pressure-controllable bleeding is consistent with a wound suitable for primary closure rather than emergency haemorrhage control.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intact finger movement and sensation, warm well-perfused hand (patient-reported)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Full active movement and intact sensation lower the probability of a divided tendon/nerve — but self-report is not a formal exam against resistance, so it cannot exclude it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Formal neurovascular examination — capillary refill <2s, sensation intact, flexor tendons intact against resistance

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Definitive exclusion of tendon/nerve injury requires in-person motor, sensory and perfusion testing — sealed clinician finding, not obtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clean wound bed on direct exploration — no retained glass foreign body

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Direct visual exploration of the wound base (± imaging for radiopaque fragments) is required to exclude retained glass before closure — an in-person step.

symptoms.symptom_name: Stable vital signs (BP 135/85, HR 98, Temp 36.8, RR 18, SpO2 100%)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal blood pressure and heart rate with no tachycardia argue against significant ongoing blood loss — clinician-measured, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Uncertain / out-of-date tetanus immunisation status in a glass wound

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Does not change the wound diagnosis but mandates a tetanus risk assessment and possible booster as part of definitive in-person care.
A glass mechanism raises the pre-test probability of a retained radiolucent fragment and of deep structure injury.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person wound exploration under local anaesthesia (direct visualisation of the wound base)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Required to exclude retained glass and deep structure injury and to enable irrigation and primary closure — a hands-on procedure the AI Doctor cannot perform.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Formal neurovascular and flexor tendon examination
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Definitively excludes tendon/nerve injury before closure; cannot be done via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Soft-tissue imaging (ultrasound/X-ray) for retained foreign body
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not routinely required when direct exploration confirms a clean wound bed; reserve for suspected deep or non-visualised fragments. The AI Doctor should not over-order imaging remotely.
